title: strychnos gardneri
intro: Strychnos gardneri, also known as the African strychnine tree or the strychnine tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, known for its toxic alkaloids, particularly strychnine, which is used as a poison and in some medical applications. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Strychnos gardneri include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to stimulate appetite and digestion. Herbal preparations of Strychnos gardneri, including extracts and decoctions, are used in traditional medicine, although caution is advised due to the plant's toxicity and potential side effects.
